tasks.description: [3/3] Runbook polish — 'Complete Runbook' button, step status transitions (all 4 states), cancel runbook, read-only completed view, progress text, runbook metadata footer, list view status filter
acceptance_criteria: 'Complete Runbook' button on runbook-view header — calls updateRunbook(id, { status: 'completed', completed_at: new Date().toISOString() }). Requires confirmation dialog before executing. Button disabled when runbook already completed/cancelled.
'Cancel Runbook' option (dropdown or secondary button) — sets status='cancelled'. Requires confirmation. No completed_at timestamp for cancellation.

Step status transitions: replace simple checkbox toggle with a dropdown or button group exposing all 4 states: pending, in_progress, completed, skipped. Completed sets completed_at, others clear it. Use existing STEP_STATUS_CONFIG for badge colors.

Completed/cancelled runbooks are read-only — step toggles disabled, notes textarea disabled, Complete/Cancel buttons hidden. Show a banner: 'This runbook was completed on {date}' or 'This runbook was cancelled'.
Progress text below progress bar: 'X of Y steps completed' with optional breakdown (e.g., '2 in progress, 1 skipped').
Runbook metadata footer: started_at date, completed_at date (if set), created_by (show 'by you' or user email if available).
Runbooks list view: add status filter tabs or dropdown (All / Active / Completed / Cancelled). Default to 'All'. Client-side filter on existing data.

patterns_to_follow: Use updateRunbook(id, { status, completed_at }) from client.ts — already exists
Use updateRunbookStep(id, { status, completed_at }) from client.ts — already exists
Status badge configs already defined in runbook-view.tsx (RUNBOOK_STATUS_CONFIG, STEP_STATUS_CONFIG)
Optimistic updates with rollback on error — existing pattern in runbook-view.tsx
Confirmation before destructive actions — use window.confirm() or dialog component
Client-side filtering — see comments-view.tsx category filter pattern
learnings_relevant: React state immutability — use [...arr].map() for updates, never mutate

tasks.special_instructions: The API already supports all needed operations (updateRunbook for status/completed_at, updateRunbookStep for status/completed_at). This is pure UI work. Do NOT modify API routes or client.ts. Step assignment UI (assigned_to) is out of scope for this sub-task — the field exists but showing a user picker is a separate feature. Focus on the 7 acceptance criteria items.

tasks.description: Add empty state to annotation panel area when no perspective is active — 'Select a perspective to add annotations' message
acceptance_criteria: When a step/section is selected on the process canvas BUT no perspective is active, show a subtle empty state message where AnnotationPanel would appear: 'Select a perspective to add annotations'. Match existing panel section styling (text-white/55, small text).
Same behavior on journey canvas (touchpoint/stage selected, no perspective active).
When a perspective IS active, AnnotationPanel renders as before (no change to existing behavior).
When nothing is selected, nothing changes (panels don't show at all).

similar_files: src/components/panels/annotation-panel.tsx — see how it renders when active
patterns_to_follow: activePerspective from useWorkspace() context — already used in both canvas views
Empty state text: text-white/55 text-sm, centered in panel area

tasks.special_instructions: Minimal change. Find the conditional render of AnnotationPanel in both canvas views (gated on activePerspective). Add an else branch with a small message div. Do NOT restructure existing panel layout or add new components — just a conditional text message.

shared_files.note: No shared file modifications needed. Both tasks are pure UI changes using existing APIs and types.
